error_message: Wrong reference: mod should be path
the trait bound `Condition: JsonSchema` is not satisfied (also `Time: JsonSchema`)
root_cause: k8s-openapi 0.23 only implements JsonSchema for Condition/Time when its `schemars` feature is enabled; the workspace had only `v1_31`.
fix: Added `schemars` to k8s-openapi features in the workspace [workspace.dependencies] table.

error_message: use of unresolved crate `serde_yaml` in crates/banlieue-api/src/bin/crdgen.rs
root_cause: `crdgen` is a feature-gated binary that depends on serde_yaml, but serde_yaml was only in [dev-dependencies] so the binary could not see it.
fix: Added `serde_yaml = { workspace = true, optional = true }` to [dependencies] and made the `crdgen` feature pull it in via `crdgen = ["dep:serde_yaml"]`.

error_message: panic in kube-core schema flattener: Property "source" has the schema [enum: "static"] but was already defined as [enum: "dhcp", description: ...]
root_cause: kube-derive's CRD schema flattener is fundamentally incompatible with `#[serde(tag = "...")]` enums. By construction each variant subschema fixes the discriminator property to a *different* enum value (`dhcp`, `static`, `pool`), and kube-core's flattener demands an identical schema for any property used in multiple subschemas. The schemars 0.8 panic surfaced a description-metadata mismatch first; on schemars 1 + kube 3 the discriminator-value mismatch is what panics. Tagged enums cannot survive CRD generation either way. Stripping doc comments removed one symptom but not the cause.
fix: FIXED 2026-05-25 by restructuring `IpamSpec` from a serde-tagged enum into a flat struct: `IpamSpec { source: IpamSource, static: Option<StaticIpamConfig>, pool: Option<PoolIpamConfig> }`. New `IpamSource` is a plain `Dhcp|Static|Pool` enum that serializes as a lower-case string. Wire format changed (sub-config now nested under a sibling field named after the discriminator), which is Kubernetes-idiomatic and matches how CAPI structures similar shapes. Cross-field validation (`source=Static implies static is set`) is intentionally NOT schema-enforced — left to the controller and/or a future CEL rule. The three `*_currently_panics_due_to_ipam_spec_bug` tests were replaced with positive CRD-metadata assertions. Verified end-to-end: `cargo run -p banlieue-api --bin crdgen --features crdgen` now emits 6 valid CRDs.

error_message: error validating "deploy/crds/banlieue-crds.yaml": error validating data: failed to download openapi: Get "http://localhost:8080/openapi/v2?timeout=32s": dial tcp [::1]:8080: connect: connection refused
root_cause: `kubectl apply --dry-run=client` performs server-side OpenAPI validation by default: it tries to fetch the cluster's /openapi/v2 to validate the manifest. In a GitHub Actions runner there is no cluster and no kubeconfig, so kubectl falls back to localhost:8080 (the legacy default) and connection-refuses. `--dry-run=client` controls *application* mode, not *validation* mode — they are independent flags.
fix: Added `--validate=false` to skip the OpenAPI fetch (kubectl still structurally parses and shape-checks the manifest), then added a `grep -c '^kind: CustomResourceDefinition'` sanity check to assert the generator produced at least one CRD. Locally verifies: 6 CRDs generated and parsed.

error_message: Error: Resource not accessible by integration - https://docs.github.com/rest/actions/workflow-runs#get-a-workflow-run (in github/codeql-action/upload-sarif step of the SAST/Semgrep job)
root_cause: `github/codeql-action/upload-sarif` calls the GitHub REST API `GET /repos/{owner}/{repo}/actions/runs/{run_id}` to gather workflow-run metadata used for SARIF fingerprinting (deduplication of repeat findings across runs). That endpoint requires the `actions:read` permission, which is NOT included in the default GITHUB_TOKEN scope and was not declared at the job level. The job had `security-events: write` (lets it upload SARIF) but not `actions: read` (lets it look up the workflow run).
fix: Added `actions: read` to the job-level `permissions:` block in sast.yaml (semgrep job), codeql.yaml (analyze job), and scorecard.yaml (analysis job — replaced the commented-out hint). All three workflows use upload-sarif and would hit the same failure on later runs.

error_message: unable to recognize "deploy/crds/banlieue-crds.yaml": Get "http://localhost:8080/api?timeout=32s": dial tcp [::1]:8080: connect: connection refused
root_cause: Sequel to bug-016. `kubectl apply --validate=false` skipped OpenAPI schema validation but kubectl still calls the cluster's `/api` discovery endpoint to *resolve* each kind/apiVersion to a REST resource (the "mapper" phase). Discovery has no offline flag in kubectl — `apply` cannot function without contacting an API server. `--dry-run=client` only controls whether the resolved operation is executed; it does not bypass discovery.
fix: Replaced the kubectl validation step entirely with an inline Python YAML validator: parse every document with yaml.safe_load_all, assert each has apiVersion=apiextensions.k8s.io/v1, kind=CustomResourceDefinition, a non-empty metadata.name, and at least one spec.versions[]. Removed the azure/setup-kubectl install step. Python 3 is preinstalled on ubuntu-latest, so no install. `kube-derive` already guarantees structural correctness at compile time, so the validator only needs to verify generator output shape — not enforce K8s-API-server semantics.

error_message: Wrong reference: vm_spec_default_power_state_is_on should be vm_spec_default_power_state_is_powered_on

The CustomResourceDefinition "virtualmachines.banlieue.io" is invalid: spec.validation.openAPIV3Schema.properties[spec].properties[desiredPowerState].default: Invalid value: "boolean": in body must be of type string: "boolean"
root_cause: PowerState enum used variant names On/Off/Suspended. The generated CRD YAML had bare `default: On` and `enum: - On - Off`. Go's YAML 1.1 parser (used by the kube apiserver) interprets bare `On`/`Off` (any case) as boolean literals, so the apiserver saw a boolean default for a string-typed field and rejected the CRD.
fix: Renamed variants to PowerState::PoweredOn / PoweredOff / Suspended (vSphere/CAPI terminology). PoweredOn/PoweredOff are unambiguous strings in YAML 1.1. Updated all call sites (virtualmachine.rs default_power_on, virtualmachine_tests.rs, common_tests.rs) and the example YAML. Regenerated deploy/crds/.

error_message: clippy: this `if` statement can be collapsed — could not compile banlieue-api / banlieue-provider-vsphere with -D warnings --all-features
root_cause: Workspace MSRV bumped to Rust 1.88, which stabilized let-chains. clippy::collapsible_if now flags nested `if let X { if Y { .. } }` that it previously allowed. Surfaced only with --all-features (crddoc is gated behind the crdgen feature).
fix: Collapsed each nested if into a let-chain: `if let X && Y { .. }` / `if let X && let Err(e) = .. { .. }`.

error_message: Wrong reference: GOVC_ should be GOVC
Wrong reference: e1cbc149bc1e9c87267d3b5c4d8c7d3b8f8e6f1a should be b9e6c61851076cb2742befb6b742537cafe31bdf
failed to read .../third_party/vim_rs/Cargo.toml; later: vim_rs does not have feature vcsim_compat; later: patch `vim_rs` was not used in the crate graph
root_cause: [patch.crates-io] must point at the crate subdir (upstream is a multi-crate repo: vim_rs/vim_rs), the gitignored checkout must be vendored before any cargo run, and the feature we need (vcsim_compat) only exists in 0.4.4 which was published to crates.io + lives on main but was NEVER git-tagged (newest tag v0.4.3 predates it). A non-exact `0.4` dep let cargo prefer crates.io 0.4.4 over the v0.4.3 patch, so the patch was ignored.
fix: Pin VIM_RS_REF to the 'Prepare 0.4.4 release' commit b9e6c61851076cb2742befb6b742537cafe31bdf (not a tag); patch path = third_party/vim_rs/vim_rs; dep pinned exactly =0.4.4 so the patch resolves; make vendor-vim-rs is a prereq of all cargo targets and runs in CI via .github/actions/vendor-vim-rs.

error_message: vim_rs recompiled (~5min) on every back-to-back make target (e.g. kind-deploy-controller then kind-deploy-provider-vsphere)
root_cause: vendor-vim-rs was .PHONY so it ran every invocation; its `git reset --hard` rewrote vendored source mtimes, which cargo's fingerprint saw as changed -> full vim_rs recompile.
fix: Move the real recipe to a file target $(VIM_RS_DIR)/.vendor-stamp with prereqs Makefile + $(wildcard patch); vendor-vim-rs becomes a .PHONY alias depending on the stamp. make skips the reset when stamp is fresh -> no mtime churn -> no recompile. Bumping VIM_RS_REF (touches Makefile) or editing the patch re-triggers.

error_message: reqwest 0.13 panic: 'No provider set' on first TLS use (vim_rs 0.5 BYOC tests + runtime)
root_cause: reqwest 0.13 with feature `rustls-no-provider` resolves the rustls CryptoProvider via CryptoProvider::get_default() and panics if none is installed. We chose rustls-no-provider+ring (to avoid aws-lc-rs cross-compile friction and match kube's ring provider) but nothing installed a process-default provider.
fix: Add install_default_crypto_provider() = rustls::crypto::ring::default_provider().install_default() (ignore Err = idempotent); call once at the top of the vsphere provider run() before any TLS (kube client + BYOC vCenter client). Tests that build a reqwest client call it first.

error_message: cargo-deny: 'failed to satisfy license requirements' — CDLA-Permissive-2.0 (webpki-root-certs) not allowed; + phf/phf_shared duplicate warnings
root_cause: reqwest 0.12→0.13 bump (ADR-0009) pulls rustls-platform-verifier → webpki-root-certs (Mozilla CA bundle as DATA, licensed CDLA-Permissive-2.0) which the allowlist lacked. Both reqwest rustls features depend on it (unavoidable). Separately vim_rs 0.5 carries phf 0.11 (vim_macros) + 0.13 (vim_rs) internally.
fix: deny.toml: add 'CDLA-Permissive-2.0' to licenses.allow (permissive data license, no copyleft); add phf@0.11.3 + phf_shared@0.11.3 to bans.skip. cargo deny check → advisories/bans/licenses/sources all ok.
